ucs.title: [LLM] Cobalt Group EQNEDT32.EXE child-process spawn (CVE-2017-11882 / 0802 Equation Editor exploit chain)
ucs.description: Hunts Cobalt Group's hallmark initial-access technique: weaponized RTF/DOC spearphishes that exploit Microsoft Equation Editor (CVE-2017-11882, CVE-2017-8570, CVE-2018-0802) and cause EQNEDT32.EXE to spawn a script/LOLBin child. EQNEDT32 is an out-of-process OLE host that has no legitimate reason to spawn a shell — any child is a near-certain exploit signal and matches the bank-targeting tradecraft of GOLD KINGSWOOD / Cobalt Spider documented by Talos, FireEye and Group-IB.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_hash) as child_hash values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name="EQNEDT32.EXE" OR Processes.parent_process_name="eqnedt32.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","hh.exe","msiexec.exe","schtasks.exe","forfiles.exe") by host Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Cobalt Group / GOLD KINGSWOOD — EQNEDT32 exploit chain (CVE-2017-11882, CVE-2018-0802)
let _suspect_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","hh.exe","msiexec.exe","schtasks.exe","forfiles.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "EQNEDT32.EXE"          // Equation Editor — out-of-process OLE host
| where FileName in~ (_suspect_children)                       // any of these = exploit shellcode payload
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cobalt Group / GOLD KINGSWOOD — EQNEDT32 exploit chain (CVE-2017-11882, CVE-2018-0802)
let _suspect_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","regsvr32.exe","rundll32.exe","bitsadmin.exe","certutil.exe","hh.exe","msiexec.exe","schtasks.exe","forfiles.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "EQNEDT32.EXE"             // Equation Editor parent
| extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where ChildBin in (_suspect_children)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
          ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Cobalt Group EQNEDT32 Child Process (CVE-2017-11882 Exploit)
id: 9b6f4a2c-cobalt-eqnedt32-child
status: stable
description: Detects Microsoft Equation Editor (EQNEDT32.EXE) spawning a script/LOLBin child — Cobalt Group / GOLD KINGSWOOD signature initial-access technique against banks since 2017.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0080/
  - https://blog.talosintelligence.com/rokrat-reloaded/
  - https://www.group-ib.com/resources/research-hub/cobalt/
author: SOC Detection Eng
date: 2026/05/04
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\EQNEDT32.EXE'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\hh.exe'
      - '\msiexec.exe'
      - '\schtasks.exe'
      - '\forfiles.exe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legacy patched-but-still-deployed Office installs running internal equation macros (extremely rare)
level: high
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1203
  - attack.t1566.001
  - attack.g0080
ucs.rationale: Cobalt Group / Cobalt Spider has used Equation Editor exploits as their primary initial-access vector against banks for years (Talos, Group-IB, Trend Micro, ESET reports all converge on this). EQNEDT32.EXE is a niche OOP OLE component that, by design, never legitimately spawns a shell or scripting host — so any child here is functionally equivalent to 'exploit succeeded'. This catches their RTF sp
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Cobalt Group Squiblydoo: regsvr32 /i: remote scrobj.dll COM scriptlet load
ucs.description: Hunts Cobalt Group's signature defense-evasion + execution combo: regsvr32.exe invoking the Squiblydoo bypass (`/s /n /u /i:<http(s)://...> scrobj.dll`) to fetch and execute a remote SCT/COM scriptlet, often delivered after the Equation Editor exploit lands. This LOLBin chain (T1218.010 + T1105) was pervasive in Cobalt Gang banking campaigns (Group-IB, Positive Technologies). Pairs the command-line shape with an outbound public connection from regsvr32 to lock fidelity high.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="regsvr32.exe" AND (Processes.process="*scrobj.dll*" OR Processes.process="*/i:http*" OR Processes.process="*/i:\\\\*") AND (Processes.process="*http://*" OR Processes.process="*https://*") by host Processes.process_name Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner host [| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic where All_Traffic.app="regsvr32.exe" AND All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port | rename All_Traffic.src as host All_Traffic.dest as remote_ip All_Traffic.dest_port as remote_port] | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
// Cobalt Group — Squiblydoo (regsvr32 + remote scrobj.dll)
let _window = 5m;
let _squiblydoo = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName =~ "regsvr32.exe"
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has "scrobj.dll"
        and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/i:\s*(https?://|\\\\)"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/s","/n","/u")     // canonical Squiblydoo flag combo
    | project SqTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              ProcessId, ProcessCommandLine,
              ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
_squiblydoo
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "regsvr32.exe"
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl,
              NetProcId = InitiatingProcessId
  ) on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (SqTime - _window .. SqTime + _window)
  and ProcessId == NetProcId
| project SqTime, NetTime, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ProcessCommandLine, ParentImage, ParentCmd,
          RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl
| order by SqTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Cobalt Group — Squiblydoo (regsvr32 + remote scrobj.dll)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| extend Bin = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where Bin == "regsvr32.exe"
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where CommandLine has "scrobj.dll"
    and CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)/i:\s*(https?://|\\\\)"
| where CommandLine has_any ("/s","/n","/u")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName,
          CommandLine, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Cobalt Group Squiblydoo Regsvr32 Remote Scriptlet
id: 7e2db1a8-cobalt-squiblydoo
status: stable
description: Detects Cobalt Group / GOLD KINGSWOOD use of regsvr32.exe with Squiblydoo flags loading a remote scrobj.dll COM scriptlet (T1218.010 + T1105).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0080/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1218/010/
  - https://www.group-ib.com/resources/research-hub/cobalt/
author: SOC Detection Eng
date: 2026/05/04
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith: '\regsvr32.exe'
  selection_scrobj:
    CommandLine|contains: 'scrobj.dll'
  selection_remote:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)/i:\s*(https?://|\\\\)'
  selection_flags:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '/s'
      - '/n'
      - '/u'
  condition: selection_image and selection_scrobj and selection_remote and selection_flags
falsepositives:
  - Bespoke admin tooling that fetches remote .sct files (very rare, allowlist by source host)
level: high
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1218.010
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.g0080
ucs.rationale: Squiblydoo (regsvr32 + remote scrobj.dll) is a defining Cobalt Group LOLBin abuse — referenced in nearly every public Cobalt Gang report from Group-IB, Positive Technologies, and Trend Micro between 2017-2020 and revived in post-arrest activity. Combining the cmdline signature with a same-PID outbound public connection from regsvr32.exe within minutes eliminates the residual FP (legit COM unregist

_field_issues.message: `Public` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`RemoteIP` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceLogonEvents, DeviceEvents, AlertEvidence, ASCDeviceEvents.
`RemotePort` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceLogonEvents, DeviceEvents, ASCDeviceEvents.
`RemoteUrl` is not a column of DeviceProcessEvents; it does exist on DeviceNetworkEvents, DeviceEvents, AlertEvidence, ASCDeviceEvents.
`SqTime` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ParentImage` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.
`ParentCmd` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents.

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
